title: Slope-Intercept Form
notes: m is the slope, b is the y-intercept.
The y-intercept is the point where the line crosses the y-axis.
Slope-intercept form is the most recognizable form for graphing quickly.
If the equation is not solved for y, rearrange it to fit y = mx + b.

examples.description: Write the line with slope 2 and y-intercept −5.

applications: Modeling linear relationships in science, economics, and data analysis.
